payload.text:
Generate a responsive, mobile-first delivery tracking page using React, Next.js, and Tailwind CSS. The design should resemble the professional user interface found in the provided image (a Lalamove/Grab style map UI).

**Layout Requirements:**

1.  **Mobile View (Default):**
    *   A full-screen container for a side menu at the top half and content area at the bottom.
    *   The map area should initially be a gray placeholder box with text that says "Map will appear here after embed".
    *   A main content area (mimicking the bottom half of the image) with driver details (Name: Emerson Teologo, Rating: 4.8, Vehicle: Motorcycle 603UEM), and "Chat" and "Call" action buttons at the bottom.
    *   A prominent "EMBED" button in the side menu or navigation bar.

2.  **Desktop View:**
    *   A standard two-column layout. The map should be visible on the left, and the delivery details sidebar on the right.

**Functionality Requirements:**

*   Implement a **modal/popup** component that appears when the user clicks the "EMBED" button.
*   The modal should contain a large `textarea` input field for pasting an iframe or script tag.
*   Once the embed script is pasted and a "Save & View Map" button is clicked (handle the state change), the gray map placeholder should be replaced by the actual embedded map.
*   Ensure the modal is accessible and closes when the backdrop is clicked or a close button is pressed.

**Design & Styling:**

*   Use clean, modern Tailwind CSS styling with rounded corners and appropriate spacing to match the aesthetic in the source image.
*   Focus on a mobile-first approach, ensuring the UI adapts gracefully to larger screens using Tailwind's breakpoints (sm:, md:, lg:).

The AI should generate the full React component code, ready for deployment on a platform like Vercel.

**Envisioning Core Features**

I'm now zeroing in on the fundamental features. Thinking about a responsive map display is crucial. For now, it's just a placeholder, but the goal is to make it interactive. I'm prioritizing user experience and efficient data visualization. This app will be an extension of existing tracking apps, and I'm keen on making it stand out.


**Developing the UI Architecture**

I'm solidifying the UI structure. The key files are now clearer, and I've sketched out the mobile-desktop layout. I'll use a side-by-side view for desktops and stacked for mobile. I'm focusing on the embed logic to safely render the map content, planning to parse the iframe's `src` using `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`. The color scheme will be vibrant orange, white, and dark, and I've planned the driver details to be displayed: photo, name, rating, vehicle.


**Outlining the Components**

I'm laying out the key UI components now. I've got `metadata.json`, the standard `index.html`, and the core `App.tsx` handling state for the embed and modal. The `EmbedModal.tsx` provides a text area and save button, while `MapArea.tsx` will display either a placeholder or the injected HTML. The `DriverDetails.tsx` will house the driver info. Mobile layout prioritizes the map, with driver details sliding up or sitting at the bottom. The desktop uses a flex row with the map expanding and the sidebar holding the driver information. I will be sure to make the embed code safe. I'll make the header sticky with the "EMBED" button and design the map as a nice placeholder. Finally, I will make all the cards rounded.


**Solidifying the Tech Stack**

I've finalized the core tech choices. React 18+ with TypeScript is confirmed, and I'll use Tailwind exclusively for styling. Responsiveness is a must. The metadata file will be integrated. I'm focusing on the "EMBED" button and am planning on placing it prominently in the header. Now, let's build!

entries.description: Application metadata containing the name and description of the delivery tracking app.
The primary HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS support.
React application entry point using the React 18 createRoot API.
Shared TypeScript interfaces for the application.
A modal component for users to paste iframe or script tags to embed a map.
The map section that displays either a placeholder or the embedded content.
The information panel showing driver details, status, and action buttons.

entries.description: Main application component managing the layout and state.

payload.text:
**Revising the App Name**

I'm currently focused on renaming the application. I've discovered the current name "SwiftTrack Delivery Pro" hardcoded within both `metadata.json` and `index.html`. My next step is to replace all instances of this name with the new moniker, "LALAM0VE". I'll then ensure all other references are accurate, and update our documentation.


**Adjusting UI Elements**

I'm now diving into the UI changes, specifically in `App.tsx`. I'll replace "SwiftTrack" with "LALAM0VE" in the mobile header and update the desktop brand logo to reflect the new name. I also need to remove the "PRO" badge, ensuring the overall look is consistent with the desired Lalamove/Grab style. I'll test the changes and adjust as needed to align the new branding with the existing specifications.

entries.description: Update the application name to LALAM0VE.
Update the HTML title to LALAM0VE.
Update brand labels from SwiftTrack to LALAM0VE in both mobile and desktop views.
